seoTitle: How to Automate Word to PDF With Power Automate
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Power Automate flow that converts new Word documents to PDF and saves, emails, or archives the result automatically.
intro: Manually converting Word documents to PDF is a small task that adds up when it happens often. This guide shows how to build a Power Automate flow that detects new Word files in OneDrive or SharePoint, converts them to PDF, and then saves or emails the finished file automatically.

steps.heading: Create a new cloud flow
steps.body: Start a new flow in Power Automate designed to run whenever a specific event happens, such as a file being added to a folder.

steps.heading: Configure the Word document trigger
steps.body: Set the trigger to watch a OneDrive or SharePoint folder for new Word documents so the conversion runs automatically as files arrive.

steps.heading: Convert the Word file to PDF
steps.body: Add a Microsoft 365 action that converts the detected Word file into a PDF format as part of the flow.

steps.heading: Save or email the PDF document
steps.body: Finish the flow by saving the converted PDF to a chosen location, or automatically emailing it to the relevant recipient for review or record keeping.

faq.question: Can this flow convert documents stored in SharePoint as well as OneDrive?
faq.answer: Yes, the trigger can be configured to watch either a OneDrive folder or a SharePoint document library for new Word files.

faq.question: Can I automatically email the converted PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, once the file is converted you can add an action to send it by email, either to yourself or another recipient, as part of the same flow.

faq.question: Is this useful for contracts and invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, this kind of automated conversion is commonly used for contracts, invoices, and reports where a finished document needs to be shared as a PDF.

faq.question: Do I need to manually trigger the conversion each time?
faq.answer: No, once the flow is set up it runs automatically whenever a new Word document appears in the watched location.
